sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Jefferies (JEF) Shares Prove Jim Cramer Was Right When He Advised Selling The Stock
feed.summary: Shares of Jefferies Financial Group Inc. (JEF) have significantly declined since Jim Cramer advised selling the stock in January 2025. The stock is down 42% since his remarks, experiencing a 5% drop over the past year and 29.5% year-to-date. Major events contributing to the decline include Jefferies' exposure to the bankrupt auto parts company First Brands and a negative jobs report earlier this year, contrasting sharply with Goldman Sachs' 60% gain in the same period.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says He Likes “Commercial Metals Very Much” But Prefers Nucor
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed his appreciation for Commercial Metals Company (NYSE:CMC) but stated a stronger preference for Nucor (NUE), suggesting Nucor could reach $200. Commercial Metals reported Q2 2026 earnings, missing EPS estimates but beating revenue targets, with management expecting $165 million to $175 million in EBITDA for FY26. Despite CMC's potential, the article suggests that certain AI stocks offer better prospects for higher and quicker returns.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Recommends Capital One for “Broader Scale and a More Durable Credit Card Franchise”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has a positive outlook on Capital One Financial Corporation (NYSE:COF), especially after its acquisition of Discover, which he believes provides broader scale and a more durable credit card franchise. He recommends Capital One as a mainstream lender with greater exposure to the mass market and expresses optimism for its future, citing potential for buybacks and successful integration with Discover. Cramer views Capital One as his "absolute favorite stock" despite acknowledging investment risks.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on IBM: “I Think the Buy on This Mild Pullback Is The Right Thing to Do”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed International Business Machines Corporation (NYSE: IBM) in the context of an Iran ceasefire relief rally, referring to the company as a "mystery." He suggested that a "mild pullback" presents a buying opportunity, despite a recent 13% decline influenced by concerns about AI competitor Anthropic. Cramer noted that IBM's business is complex, but its underlying performance is strong, even as competition from AI innovations raises questions for investors.

feed.title: Trading the Move, Not the Narrative: (JHPI) Edition
feed.summary: This article analyzes John Hancock Preferred Income Etf (NASDAQ: JHPI) using AI models, highlighting a strong near-term sentiment but a neutral mid and long-term outlook. It identifies a mid-channel oscillation pattern and offers three distinct trading strategies—Position, Momentum Breakout, and Risk Hedging—along with specific entry, target, and stop-loss levels. The analysis emphasizes a favorable 5.6:1 risk-reward setup, targeting a 1.7% gain against a 0.3% risk.

feed.title: A Look At U.S. Bancorp’s Valuation As It Becomes Official Bank Sponsor Of The NFL
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp (USB) has become the official bank and wealth management sponsor of the NFL, a multi-year partnership including Super Bowl MVP presenting rights and expanded player financial education. The company's share price has seen recent gains and has a one-year total shareholder return of 53.3%. Simply Wall St's analysis suggests a fair value of $58.09, indicating the stock is currently undervalued at its trading price of $55.66.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says He Does Not “Have a Reason to Recommend General Mills”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer reiterated his negative stance on General Mills (NYSE: GIS), despite its high dividend yield, stating he sees no reason to recommend the stock. He cited the stock's continued decline despite its yield and a recent quarter that wasn't strong enough to halt its fall. Cramer also advised that certain AI stocks might offer greater upside potential with less risk than GIS.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says Goldman Sachs’ Upcoming Earnings Report “Should Be a Good One”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has expressed optimism about Goldman Sachs' (NYSE: GS) upcoming earnings report, citing multiple reasons to own the stock, including potential for takeovers and advisory business. He stated that the stock is "ridiculously down" and "way too cheap" at 15 times earnings. While acknowledging Goldman Sachs' potential, the article also suggests that certain AI stocks might offer greater upside and less downside risk.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Dow Inc. as One of the Stocks “Benefiting From the Man-Made Petrochemical Shortage”
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed how Dow Inc. (NYSE: DOW) and LyondellBasell are benefiting from a "man-made petrochemical shortage" that he attributes to the war and potential disruptions from Iran. He noted that these commodity chemical companies, despite being poor performers last year, have seen significant gains this year. Cramer suggests holding these stocks, acknowledging the risk of a pullback if tensions ease but also the possibility of continued shortages.

feed.title: Here’s why the SPY ETF has shed $2o billion as VOO and SPYM rake $66b
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 (SPY) ETF has seen over $20 billion in outflows this year, while the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) and State Street SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 ETF (SPYM) have collectively gained $66 billion. This rotation is primarily driven by the lower expense ratios of VOO (0.03%) and SPYM (0.02%) compared to SPY (0.09%), as all three ETFs track the S&P 500 with similar returns. Investors are opting for the lower-fee alternatives to maximize returns over time.

feed.title: Digital Realty’s Big Singapore AI Bet Might Change The Case For Investing In Digital Realty Trust (DLR)
feed.summary: Digital Realty has significantly expanded its Asia-Pacific presence with a new AI-ready data center in Japan and a substantial S$7.00 billion investment plan for Singapore, focusing on AI infrastructure and a regional innovation lab. This strategic move, combined with securing a US$3.25 billion hyperscale data center fund, aims to boost capital-efficient growth and cater to advanced AI and hybrid cloud demands. The article suggests these developments could reshape Digital Realty's investment outlook, though financing costs and valuation remain key considerations.

feed.title: Here’s why the SPY ETF has shed $2o billion as VOO and SPYM rake $66b
feed.summary: The SPY ETF has experienced significant outflows, shedding over $20 billion this year, while Vanguard's VOO and State Street's SPYM ETFs have collectively gained $66 billion. This shift is primarily attributed to the lower expense ratios of VOO (0.03%) and SPYM (0.02%) compared to SPY (0.09%), prompting investors to seek better value when tracking the S&P 500 Index. The S&P 500 Index itself is nearing its all-time high, with future performance potentially influenced by US-Iran talks and upcoming earnings reports from major companies.

feed.title: Here’s why the SPY ETF has shed $2o billion as VOO and SPYM rake $66b
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 (SPY) ETF has seen significant outflows this year, losing over $20 billion, as investors shift to its competitors, the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) and the State Street SPDR Portfolio S&P 500 ETF (SPYM). This rotation is primarily driven by the lower expense ratios of VOO and SPYM (0.03% and 0.02% respectively) compared to SPY's 0.09%, leading to substantial inflows into the Vanguard and State Street funds. Despite the small fee difference, investors are prioritizing cost efficiency as all three ETFs track the S&P 500 and offer similar returns.

feed.title: Southwest (LUV) Shares Up Since Jim Cramer Advised Selling The Stock
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (LUV) shares have risen significantly since Jim Cramer advised selling the stock in April 2025, with gains of 56% overall, including a 79% jump between October 31st and February 6th. Despite an 18.7% surge on January 29th after announcing a minimum $4 adjustable profit per share for 2026, the stock recently dipped by 6% due to oil market turmoil. Cramer's initial recommendation was based on the cyclical nature of airline stocks, but he later acknowledged the company's turnaround efforts with Elliott Management.

feed.title: Hershey’s Sweet Surrender: Classic Reese’s Chocolate Returns In 2027
feed.summary: Hershey has announced that it will revert select Reese's products to their classic milk and dark chocolate recipes starting in 2027. This decision follows public criticism and will affect about 3% of the Reese's lineup that previously used a lower-chocolate coating, while core products like Reese's Peanut Butter Cups, which always used the original recipe, will remain unchanged. The company also confirmed broader efforts to enhance other products like Kit Kat recipes and transition to natural colors across its sweets portfolio.

feed.title: Enphase (ENPH) Stock Down Signficantly After Jim Cramer Advised Selling It
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) stock has significantly declined after Jim Cramer advised selling it, primarily due to regulatory concerns in the renewable energy sector and disappointing earnings guidance. The solar power technology company's shares are down 36% over the past year and further dropped 43% since Cramer's comments on April 2nd, driven by potential phase-outs of solar and wind energy credits and lower-than-expected revenue forecasts. Cramer expressed skepticism about the company's political support, suggesting no good news was likely for Enphase.

feed.title: Can you recognize the questionable symbol Coca-Cola chose to represent Oregon in its new states collection?
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is releasing a collection of 50 state-themed mini cans to celebrate the United States' 250th birthday, each featuring "iconic local symbols." The Oregon can design, however, depicts the Oregon grape flower in a simplified silhouette, which proved difficult for many Oregonians to recognize since it is not instantly identifiable. The article discusses other potential Oregon symbols that might have been more recognizable for the collection.

feed.summary: New to The Street will broadcast a new episode tonight at 6:30 PM EST on Bloomberg Television, featuring executive interviews and company insights from IGC Pharma, Vivos Therapeutics, Acurx Pharmaceuticals, and Equinox Gold. The broadcast also includes a continuing M&A segment with Frank Aquila from Sullivan & Cromwell LLP. New to The Street distinguishes itself with national television exposure, a large YouTube audience, and prominent outdoor billboard placements in Times Square and the New York Financial District.

feed.title: Gyrodyne Short Interest Surges Over 1,600% in March
feed.summary: Gyrodyne, LLC (NASDAQ:GYRO) experienced a massive 1,646.7% increase in short interest on its stock during March, with shares sold short rising from 45 to 786. This significant rise signals growing investor skepticism about the real estate firm's future performance, potentially driven by concerns over its portfolio or market conditions. The increased short interest could lead to higher stock volatility and potential short squeezes.

feed.title: How Highway 101’s Final US$114 Million Segment Win At Granite Construction (GVA) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Granite Construction (GVA) recently secured a US$114 million contract for the final segment of the Highway 101 project, reinforcing its already substantial backlog. While this win confirms Granite's integrated materials advantage and multi-year infrastructure workload, it doesn't fundamentally alter the investment thesis which centers on the company's ability to execute large projects profitably amidst cost inflation and funding cycles. Investors should monitor project costs and regional funding shifts as potential challenges to Granite’s margin gains despite an overall positive outlook for revenue and earnings growth.

feed.title: A self-proclaimed “Permian nerd” looks ahead at ConocoPhillips
feed.summary: Aaron Hunter, the president of ConocoPhillips' Permian business, expresses his passion for the region and its future prospects, highlighting the company's significant inventory and focus on innovation for enhanced recovery and capital efficiency. He emphasizes the Permian's role at the forefront of industry advancements and ConocoPhillips' commitment to community initiatives and conservation. Hunter, a self-proclaimed "Permian nerd," also looks forward to mentoring the next generation of leaders.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries Inc Stock Short Interest Rises to 39.94%

Hedge fund activity showed mixed sentiment, with 175 institutional investors adding shares and 151 decreasing positions in Q4 2025. Insider trading analysis reveals five sales and no purchases in the last six months, while analysts have issued both buy and sell ratings with a median target price of $47.5.

feed.title: IMMUNITYBIO Deadline to Lead in Securities Class Action Lawsuit is May 26, 2026 - Contact Kaplan Fox
feed.summary: Kaplan Fox & Kilsheimer LLP has announced a class action lawsuit against ImmunityBio, Inc. (NASDAQ: IBRX) for investors who purchased securities between January 19, 2026, and March 24, 2026. The lawsuit stems from a warning letter issued by the FDA on March 24, 2026, stating that ImmunityBio's TV ad and podcast for Anktiva were "false or misleading." Investors have until May 26, 2026, to move the court to serve as a lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Microsoft SharePoint Venom Attack Targets C-Suite Executives By Name
feed.summary: A new, previously undocumented phishing-as-a-service platform called "Venom" is targeting C-suite executives by name, exploiting Microsoft SharePoint. The highly sophisticated attacks use personalized emails impersonating SharePoint notifications, complete with QR codes leading to credential harvesting pages that are indistinguishable from genuine sign-in experiences. Venom is distributed through closed, vetted criminal channels and is designed to bypass automated analysis and MFA, making it a significant threat to corporate security.

feed.title: CMG: 2026 Sales Initiatives And Buybacks Will Support Higher P E Multiple
feed.summary: Analysts have slightly trimmed Chipotle Mexican Grill's price target due to marginally lower fair value and softer growth assumptions, but this is partly offset by a higher future P/E multiple. Bullish views highlight sales-driving initiatives and brand strength, while bearish concerns include execution risks and management transitions. The company recently completed significant share repurchases in late 2025 and issued 2026 guidance for flat comparable restaurant sales, alongside plans to reintroduce Chicken al Pastor and accelerate its menu schedule.

feed.title: U.S. Bank: NFL Partnership Expands Wealth Management And Financial Education Offering

The partnership aims to promote financial stability, innovation, and community impact.

Investors are watching to see if this is an isolated incident or a trend among major shareholders.

feed.title: Third Point gives up proxy battle at CoStar, sells stake: Reuters
feed.summary: Third Point, the activist hedge fund led by Daniel Loeb, has reportedly given up its proxy battle with CoStar Group and sold its stake in the company. This decision comes after CoStar Group's stock price recently fell, which likely influenced Third Point's move. The hedge fund had previously urged CoStar to improve its governance and consider strategic alternatives.

feed.title: United Therapeutics price target raised to $519 from $471 at Morgan Stanley

This adjustment is based on updated models incorporating IQVIA trends and intra-quarter updates in anticipation of Q1 earnings reports from biopharma companies. The news was initially published by TheFly.

feed.title: Xcel Energy VPP Expansion Approved in Minnesota for 200 MW Battery Storage - News and Statistics
feed.summary: The Minnesota Public Utilities Commission approved Xcel Energy's proposal for a 200-megawatt expansion of its virtual power plant program, Capacity Connect, by deploying utility-owned, community-based battery storage systems by 2028. This move has drawn criticism from solar and storage industry groups, who argue that the model monopolizes battery ownership for Xcel, sidelines local businesses, and lacks transparency in its financial evaluations compared to more competitive and effective programs in other states. Xcel Energy aims for this expansion to meet rising electricity demand, ensure reliability, optimize infrastructure, and support clean energy goals despite concerns raised about its execution and market impact.

feed.title: Why ITT (ITT) Is Up 11.7% After Surging Revenue And Free Cash Flow Margin Expansion
feed.summary: ITT has seen its revenue grow 9.7% annually over the last five years, outperforming its sector, with projections for 35% revenue growth in the next 12 months. The company has also significantly increased its free cash flow margin by 17.6 percentage points over the same period, indicating strong customer resonance and financial flexibility. Analysts forecast ITT's revenue to reach $6.3 billion and earnings to hit $858.0 million by 2029, suggesting a potential 7% upside to its current price.
